qa.overall_assessment: BA resolved all 4 prior objections (OBJ-1 through OBJ-4). Two new blocking objections remain: (NEW-1) AC6 says 'uncapped_delta == 101' while M8 says 'unclamped_score == 101' — different field names, only one can be correct; (NEW-2) M8 requires 'full body rewrite' but also 'preserve docstring verbatim' — contradictory since the docstring describes obsolete agent-scores.json semantics. One additional gap: M6 prescribes malformed JSONL to exit 1 but no AC formally verifies this behavior. BA must fix all three before Dev dispatch.
qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Score-update CAS + lifecycle log: replace direct score writes with append-only log entries {ts, agent, event, prev_score, new_score, delta, actor, reason}. Reads use latest entry; writes use Compare-And-Swap on prev_score. User observed score drift dev 81->73 between events with no event-log explanation.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: spec Section 7 Cycle 4 verbatim; BA scope contract B

prior_objection_resolutions.title: JSONL field 'note' vs 'reason'
prior_objection_resolutions.evidence: M1 now explicitly states 'rename emitted JSON field from note to reason'. JSONL schema examples show reason field. AC1 THEN clause explicitly states 'No field named note appears in the emitted JSON'. score-update.sh line 171 still emits 'note' (pre-implementation), confirming the rename is genuine pending work.

prior_objection_resolutions.title: Schema over-expanded with mandatory non-R9 fields
prior_objection_resolutions.evidence: JSONL Entry Schema now shows exactly 8 mandatory fields: ts, agent, event, prev_score, new_score, delta, actor, reason. event_id/seq/prev_event_id/kind now marked 'Advisory implementation-only fields (NOT verified by ACs, NOT mandatory JSONL)'. Batch fields removed entirely. Context JSON scope.excluded confirms 'event_id/seq/prev_event_id as mandatory JSONL schema fields (advisory only — OBJ-2 fix)'.

prior_objection_resolutions.title: agent-scores.json dual-write retained post-migration
prior_objection_resolutions.evidence: M1 now explicitly states 'Do NOT retain the agent-scores.json write: lifecycle.jsonl is the sole score source of truth post-migration. The agent-scores.json write path in score-update.sh MUST be removed.' M3 says score-inject.sh reads lifecycle.jsonl under shared flock directly. Won't Have section confirms this. Context JSON scope.excluded includes 'agent-scores.json retained as parallel write target (eliminated — OBJ-3 fix)'.

prior_objection_resolutions.title: M5/AC8 non-discriminating gitignore check
prior_objection_resolutions.evidence: M5 is eliminated entirely. Technical Hints confirms '.gitignore NO CHANGES NEEDED'. Former AC8 became AC9 (preflight precondition) with --error-unmatch flag making it discriminating. New AC8 requires lifecycle.jsonl to have exactly 21 lines on first run — genuine new work. Verified: 'git check-ignore -v logs/lifecycle.jsonl.lock' returns '.gitignore:46:logs/*  logs/lifecycle.jsonl.lock' confirming lock file already covered without explicit entry.

objections.title: AC6 and M8 use different field names for the pre-clamp target: 'uncapped_delta' vs 'unclamped_score'
objections.ba_claim_ac6: AC6 THEN clause: 'the test asserts uncapped_delta == 101 and delta == 1'
objections.ba_claim_m8: M8 requirement: 'assert that the last line has delta == 1 (corrected from 8) and unclamped_score == 101 (corrected from 108; pre-clamp target = prev_score + delta = 100 + 1 = 101)'
objections.ba_claim_schema: JSONL Schema advisory notes: 'unclamped_score: pre-clamp arithmetic target; useful for audit but not in the mandatory schema'
objections.problem: AC6 and M8 both refer to the pre-clamp target value (100 + 1 = 101), but they use different field names: AC6 says 'uncapped_delta' while M8 says 'unclamped_score'. The existing score-update.sh emits 'uncapped_delta' at line 170. A dev who writes the test to satisfy AC6 (checking 'uncapped_delta') will write code that fails M8's 'unclamped_score' requirement and vice versa. The AC literal text and the M8 implementation specification cannot both be correct simultaneously. Context JSON success_criteria also uses 'unclamped_score', making three locations inconsistent.
objections.evidence: AC6 THEN: 'uncapped_delta == 101'. M8: 'unclamped_score == 101'. scripts/score-update.sh line 170: '"uncapped_delta": uncapped_delta'. Schema advisory: 'unclamped_score: pre-clamp arithmetic target'. Context JSON success_criteria: 'asserts delta==1 and unclamped_score==101'.
objections.required_fix: Standardize on 'unclamped_score' everywhere: update AC6 THEN clause from 'uncapped_delta == 101' to 'unclamped_score == 101'; update Technical Hints test_AC4 note to match; update context JSON success_criteria to match. Choose 'unclamped_score' because: (1) semantically correct — it is the score before clamping, not the delta itself; (2) already used in M8 and schema advisory note.
objections.codex_confirmation: Codex finding #1 (BLOCKING): 'AC6 requires uncapped_delta == 101, while M8/context require unclamped_score == 101; schema lists unclamped_score as advisory. These cannot all be true. PROPOSED_FIX: Standardize on one name everywhere. Prefer unclamped_score.'

objections.title: M8 internally contradicts itself: 'full body rewrite' vs 'preserve docstring verbatim'
objections.ba_claim: M8 requires (1) 'Rewrite the test body entirely to target lifecycle JSONL append semantics' AND (2) 'The test docstring and metadata (AC_UID = "7d4395a2a5e37239", AC_TYPE = "hook") MUST be preserved verbatim'. Technical Hints also state 'only the assertion values change' — contradicting 'Rewrite the test body entirely'.
objections.problem: The existing test_AC4 docstring says: 'GIVEN: scripts/score-update.sh has been modified to compute and append uncapped_delta AND a temporary mock scores file exists with ba score at 100 / WHEN: score-update.sh invoked with --scores-file /tmp/test-scores.json / THEN: appended history entry contains key uncapped_delta = 108... AND ts/event/delta/old_score/new_score/note fields unchanged'. This docstring describes agent-scores.json history semantics, uncapped_delta == 108, delta == 8, and the 'note' field — all obsolete after lifecycle JSONL rewrite. If the docstring 'MUST be preserved verbatim', it will describe the wrong pre-migration behavior. If the test body 'is rewritten entirely', the old docstring must also change. The Technical Hints claim 'only the assertion values change' directly contradicts 'Rewrite the test body entirely'.
objections.evidence: test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py lines 15-19: docstring references uncapped_delta, delta=8, --scores-file agent-scores.json format, 'note' field. M8 text: 'Rewrite the test body entirely'. M8 text: 'The test docstring and metadata (AC_UID = "7d4395a2a5e37239", AC_TYPE = "hook") MUST be preserved verbatim'. Technical Hints: 'only the assertion values change'.
objections.required_fix: Change M8 to: 'Preserve AC_UID = "7d4395a2a5e37239" and AC_TYPE = "hook" constants verbatim. Rewrite the test body AND docstring to lifecycle JSONL semantics (updated GIVEN/WHEN/THEN reflecting temp lifecycle file, delta == 1, unclamped_score == 101).' Remove the Technical Hints line 'only the assertion values change' — the docstring, setup, and assertions all change.
objections.codex_confirmation: Codex finding #2 (BLOCKING): 'M8 says fully rewrite test_AC4 for lifecycle JSONL, but also says preserve the old docstring verbatim; technical hints say only assertion values change. PROPOSED_FIX: Change M8 to: Preserve AC_UID and AC_TYPE only. Rewrite the test body and docstring to lifecycle JSONL semantics.'

objections.title: M6 malformed JSONL exit code 1 behavior is prescribed but no AC formally verifies it
objections.ba_claim: M6 states: 'For the malformed branch: the new expected behavior is exit code 1 with stderr message — NOT emission of an INJECTION_PROOF block. The malformed branch test expectation must change accordingly: assert exit code 1 and non-empty stderr.' The AC list (AC1-AC9) contains no AC with a THEN clause asserting exit code 1 for malformed JSONL.
objections.problem: M6 describes a behavior change (malformed JSONL now exits 1, not silently falls back) that is a Must Have requirement. Without an explicit AC, a Dev who updates the fixture but forgets to invert the exit code expectation will still pass all listed ACs. M3 specifies 'Malformed JSONL: exit 1 with stderr message' but no numbered AC verifies this. Current test-inject-branches.sh line 121 expects all 3 branches including malformed to pass.
objections.evidence: Reviewed AC1 through AC9. None has a THEN clause verifying exit code 1 on malformed JSONL. M6 in Must Have section describes the behavior change. M3 specifies the behavior. Current test-inject-branches.sh: 'run_branch malformed $MALFORMED || ec=1' where run_branch expects INJECTION_PROOF (not exit 1).
objections.required_fix: Add AC10: 'GIVEN logs/lifecycle.jsonl contains at least one valid JSON line followed by a newline-terminated invalid JSON non-final line / WHEN bash scripts/score-inject.sh --agent dev is invoked / THEN exit code is 1, stderr is non-empty, stdout does not contain INJECTION_PROOF'.
objections.codex_confirmation: Codex finding #3 (BLOCKING): 'M6 correctly requires malformed JSONL to exit 1 with stderr and no INJECTION_PROOF, but no AC verifies this. Dev could skip it and still satisfy listed ACs. PROPOSED_FIX: Add an AC.'

codex_findings_disposition.title: M6 malformed branch lacks AC coverage
CAS is optional but user requirement says writes use CAS
codex_findings_disposition.rationale: The user requirement 'writes use CAS on prev_score' is satisfied by implementing CAS as an available mechanism. R3 acceptance criterion confirms CAS is implemented and testable (AC2). Making it mandatory for ALL callers would break existing callers in commands/close.md and commands/dev.md — scope expansion beyond R9. The append-only log (M1) addresses the root cause; CAS addresses concurrent drift detection when callers opt in. Deliberate documented design trade-off, not a spec defect.

codex_findings_disposition.title: AC3 does not verify latest-entry-wins with two same-agent entries
codex_findings_disposition.rationale: AC3 verifies the core claim. Testing with two lifecycle entries is a quality improvement, not a requirement gap. The R2 user requirement is satisfied by M3 specification and AC3.

codex_findings_disposition.title: agent-scores.json write removal not AC-tested
codex_findings_disposition.rationale: M7(e) covers 'score-inject.sh reads lifecycle.jsonl and ignores agent-scores.json'. M1 explicitly says 'MUST be removed'. Absence of a specific byte-check AC is a test quality gap, not a blocking spec defect.

The agent-scores.json write path in score-update.sh MUST be removed.' Won't Have section confirms. M3 requires score-inject.sh to read lifecycle.jsonl directly.

New AC8 requires lifecycle.jsonl to have exactly 21 lines on first run — genuine new work. Verified: 'git check-ignore -v logs/lifecycle.jsonl.lock' returns '.gitignore:46:logs/*' confirming lock file already covered.

BA claims score-update.sh is called from commands/close.md lines 415-459: confirmed by grep. BA claims commands/dev.md calls score-inject.sh at lines 348, 652, 810, 897-904: confirmed by grep. BA claims flock at score-update.sh lines 63-68: confirmed (exec 9>${LOCK_FILE} and flock -w 10 9).

No scope narrowing detected on these items.

lifecycle.jsonl is already git-tracked: confirmed by git ls-files showing '100644 e69de29bb2d1d6434b8b29ae775ad8c2e48c5391 0  logs/lifecycle.jsonl'. This was committed in cycle 20260522-000000 (commit af96c61). BA's statement that 'lifecycle.jsonl was created and tracked in git by Cycle 20260522-000000' is accurate.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex iteration 2 identified 6 issues: (1) AC6 requires uncapped_delta==101 but M8/context require unclamped_score==101 — cannot both be true; (2) M8 says fully rewrite test_AC4 for lifecycle JSONL but also says preserve old docstring verbatim; Technical Hints say 'only assertion values change' contradicting full rewrite; (3) M6 requires malformed JSONL to exit 1 with stderr and no INJECTION_PROOF, but no AC verifies this; Dev could skip and still pass all listed ACs; (4) CAS is optional but user requirement says writes use CAS — making it mandatory for all callers would break close.md and dev.md; (5) AC3 does not verify latest-entry-wins with two same-agent entries; (6) agent-scores.json write removal not AC-tested.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex findings 1, 2, 3 incorporated as blocking objections NEW-1, NEW-2, NEW-3. Codex findings 4, 5, 6 rejected or marked advisory: finding 4 (CAS mandatory) is out_of_scope — CAS IS implemented as opt-in per R3; making mandatory breaks existing callers and is scope expansion; finding 5 (AC3 two-entry test) is in_scope_minor — quality improvement, not blocking; finding 6 (agent-scores.json removal not AC-tested) is in_scope_minor — M1 explicitly prescribes removal, M7(e) covers indirect test.
codex_observations_only: AC3 does not verify latest-entry-wins with two same-agent lifecycle entries
agent-scores.json write removal not directly AC-tested (only via M7(e) indirect)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex finding #4 (iteration 2, advisory): CAS is opt-in per R3 acceptance criteria. Making it mandatory for all callers (close.md, dev.md) would break existing integrations and is scope expansion beyond R9. Deliberate design decision — not a spec defect.

Codex finding #5 (iteration 2, advisory): AC3 verifies latest-entry-wins claim but only with a single lifecycle entry. Testing with two same-agent entries would be stronger evidence. Quality improvement, not blocking.

Codex finding #6 (iteration 2, advisory): agent-scores.json write removal has no dedicated AC explicitly testing that the write path is absent (e.g., byte-diff or grep of score-update.sh). M1 prescribes removal and M7(e) indirectly covers it via score-inject.sh reading lifecycle.jsonl. Minor test coverage gap.

Minor off-by-one in BA line number claims: BA says score-update.sh is 'lines 1-196' but actual file is 195 lines. BA says score-inject.sh is 'lines 1-147' but actual is 146 lines. Non-blocking; content claims are accurate.

dimension_results.detail: Iteration 2: BA's factual claims are backed by architect runtime inspection (docs/dev/architect-report-dev-20260525-050824.json) and Codex session 019e5dc7. All 4 prior-iteration defects are confirmed as genuine pending work by file-read evidence: score-update.sh line 171 emits 'note' (OBJ-1 real), JSONL schema now shows exactly 8 mandatory fields (OBJ-2 fixed in spec), M1 explicitly says 'MUST be removed' for agent-scores.json write path (OBJ-3 fixed in spec), M5 eliminated and git check-ignore confirms lock file already covered (OBJ-4 fixed). Minor off-by-one line-count claims (195 vs 196) are negligible and non-blocking.

Iteration 2: OBJ-1/2/3 scope violations resolved. JSONL schema now shows exactly {ts, agent, event, prev_score, new_score, delta, actor, reason}. M1 explicitly removes agent-scores.json write path. M3 requires score-inject.sh to read lifecycle.jsonl directly. 'reason' field now throughout. No expansionist mandatory fields remain. Scope now aligns with R9 verbatim requirement.

Iteration 2: OBJ-4 is resolved (M5 eliminated, gitignore checked). NEW-3 is a new investigation gap: M6 prescribes malformed JSONL to exit code 1 with stderr, but no AC formally verifies this behavior. M3 specifies the behavior, M6 requires test changes, but neither maps to a numbered AC with a THEN clause. Dev could skip or incorrectly implement this and still pass all listed ACs. This is a Must Have requirement (M6) that lacks AC coverage.

Iteration 2: All affected file paths verified. score-update.sh 195 lines, emits 'note' line 171 and 'uncapped_delta' line 170 (pre-implementation, confirmed). score-inject.sh 146 lines, reads agent-scores.json (pre-implementation, confirmed). lifecycle.jsonl 0 bytes git-tracked confirmed. doc_sync tree.py and regen_readme.py lack lifecycle.jsonl in SKIP sets (M4 genuine work confirmed). test-inject-branches.sh uses JSON format and expects all 3 branches to pass (M6 genuine work confirmed). test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py asserts uncapped_delta==108 and delta==8 with 'note' field (M8 genuine work confirmed).

Iteration 2: OBJ-4 drift resolved (AC9 now uses --error-unmatch flag). NEW-1 is a blocking new drift: AC6 THEN clause (ticket line 251) says 'uncapped_delta == 101' but M8 (ticket line 88) says 'unclamped_score == 101'. These are different field names. JSONL schema advisory lists 'unclamped_score'. score-update.sh currently emits 'uncapped_delta'. Context JSON success_criteria uses 'unclamped_score'. Dev following M8 will produce code that fails AC6's literal test recipe and vice versa. NEW-2 is a secondary drift: M8 says 'Rewrite the test body entirely' but also 'The test docstring...MUST be preserved verbatim' — the existing docstring describes obsolete agent-scores.json semantics and cannot be preserved if the test is rewritten for lifecycle JSONL.

failures.issue: NEW-1: AC6 THEN clause says 'uncapped_delta == 101' while M8 says 'unclamped_score == 101'. These are different field names. Dev following M8 will write code testing 'unclamped_score'; AC6's literal verification recipe tests 'uncapped_delta'. A dev cannot satisfy both simultaneously. Context JSON success_criteria also uses 'unclamped_score', making three spec locations inconsistent.
failures.recommendation: Standardize on 'unclamped_score' everywhere (semantically correct — it is the pre-clamp score, not the delta). Update AC6 THEN clause to 'unclamped_score == 101'. Update Technical Hints test_AC4 note to match. Context JSON success_criteria already uses 'unclamped_score'.

failures.issue: NEW-2: M8 internally contradicts itself. (1) 'Rewrite the test body entirely to target lifecycle JSONL append semantics' — full rewrite required. (2) 'The test docstring and metadata (AC_UID = "7d4395a2a5e37239", AC_TYPE = "hook") MUST be preserved verbatim' — the existing docstring references agent-scores.json semantics, uncapped_delta==108, delta==8, 'note' field, all obsolete. (3) Technical Hints: 'only the assertion values change' — directly contradicts 'Rewrite the test body entirely'. All three claims cannot be simultaneously true.
failures.recommendation: Change M8 to: 'Preserve AC_UID = "7d4395a2a5e37239" and AC_TYPE = "hook" constants verbatim. Rewrite the test body AND docstring entirely to lifecycle JSONL semantics (new GIVEN/WHEN/THEN reflecting temp lifecycle file, delta == 1, unclamped_score == 101).' Remove the Technical Hints sentence 'only the assertion values change'.

failures.issue: NEW-3: M6 prescribes malformed JSONL should exit code 1 with stderr (not emit INJECTION_PROOF). Current test-inject-branches.sh expects all 3 branches including malformed to pass. M3 specifies 'Malformed JSONL: exit 1 with stderr message'. No numbered AC (AC1-AC9) has a THEN clause verifying exit code 1 on malformed JSONL input. Dev could update the test fixture format to JSONL but leave the malformed branch assertion as is and still pass all ACs.
failures.recommendation: Add AC10: 'GIVEN logs/lifecycle.jsonl contains a valid JSONL preamble followed by a malformed (non-JSON) line / WHEN bash scripts/score-inject.sh --agent dev is invoked / THEN exit code is 1, stderr is non-empty, stdout does not contain INJECTION_PROOF'.

all_findings.issue: NEW-1: AC6 says 'uncapped_delta == 101', M8 says 'unclamped_score == 101' — two different field names for same test assertion
all_findings.recommendation: Standardize on 'unclamped_score' in AC6 THEN clause and Technical Hints

all_findings.issue: NEW-2: M8 contradicts itself — 'full body rewrite' vs 'preserve docstring verbatim' vs 'only assertion values change'
all_findings.recommendation: Preserve only AC_UID and AC_TYPE constants. Rewrite test body AND docstring to lifecycle JSONL semantics. Remove 'only assertion values change' from Technical Hints.

all_findings.issue: NEW-3: M6 prescribes malformed JSONL exit code 1 but no AC verifies this behavior
all_findings.recommendation: Add AC10 testing exit code 1 + non-empty stderr + no INJECTION_PROOF on malformed JSONL input

summary.verdict_reason: 4 prior objections (OBJ-1/2/3/4) fully resolved. 3 new blocking defects: NEW-1 AC6 vs M8 field name conflict (uncapped_delta vs unclamped_score); NEW-2 M8 'full rewrite' vs 'preserve verbatim' internal contradiction; NEW-3 M6 malformed JSONL exit-1 behavior has no AC coverage. BA must fix all 3 before Dev dispatch.

critical_issues: NEW-1: Standardize field name to 'unclamped_score' in AC6 THEN clause (currently says 'uncapped_delta == 101'). M8 and context JSON already use 'unclamped_score'.

NEW-2: Fix M8 self-contradiction. Preserve only AC_UID and AC_TYPE constants. Rewrite both test body AND docstring for lifecycle JSONL semantics. Remove 'only assertion values change' from Technical Hints.
major_issues: NEW-3: Add AC10 testing that malformed JSONL input causes score-inject.sh to exit 1 with non-empty stderr and no INJECTION_PROOF output.
refined_context.recommended_approach: BA must produce a corrected ticket addressing NEW-1/2/3 before Dev dispatch. The core R9 architecture (JSONL append, CAS, migration, doc_sync exclusions, 8-field schema, agent-scores.json write removal, test corrections) is now correctly specified. Only the AC6 field-name typo, M8 docstring contradiction, and M6 missing AC need correction.
refined_context.additional_context: Codex iteration 2 independently confirmed all 3 new findings. Iteration 1 objections OBJ-1 through OBJ-4 are verified resolved. lifecycle.jsonl JSONL schema: exactly 8 R9-verbatim mandatory fields. agent-scores.json write: explicitly removed by M1. score-inject.sh: correctly routed to lifecycle.jsonl by M3.

spec_section_updates.section_4: Cycle 4 BA-validation iteration 2: Prior objections OBJ-1/2/3/4 verified resolved. Iteration 2 measured state: score-update.sh (195 lines) currently emits 'uncapped_delta' (line 170) and 'note' (line 171) — pre-implementation, both are genuine pending work confirmed in BA spec. JSONL schema now exactly 8 fields. M1 explicitly removes agent-scores.json write. M3 routes score-inject.sh to lifecycle.jsonl. AC6 THEN clause (line 251): 'uncapped_delta == 101' — inconsistent with M8 (line 88): 'unclamped_score == 101'. M8 contains self-contradictory directives: 'Rewrite the test body entirely' vs 'The test docstring MUST be preserved verbatim' vs Technical Hints 'only the assertion values change'. M6 prescribes exit code 1 for malformed JSONL but no AC1-AC9 verifies this. lifecycle.jsonl: 0 bytes (tracked). test_AC4: stale (uncapped_delta==108, delta==8, note field).
spec_section_updates.section_6: Cycle 4 BA-validation iteration 2: NEW-1 — AC6 THEN clause uses 'uncapped_delta == 101' but M8 implementation spec uses 'unclamped_score == 101'; these are different field names; a dev cannot simultaneously satisfy both; context JSON success_criteria also uses 'unclamped_score'. NEW-2 — M8 directs 'Rewrite the test body entirely' for lifecycle JSONL semantics while simultaneously directing 'The test docstring...MUST be preserved verbatim'; the existing docstring references obsolete agent-scores.json semantics (uncapped_delta==108, delta==8, note field); Technical Hints compound the confusion with 'only the assertion values change'. NEW-3 — M6 prescribes malformed JSONL input causes exit code 1 with stderr, but no numbered AC in AC1-AC9 has a THEN clause testing for exit code 1 on malformed JSONL.
spec_section_updates.section_7: Cycle 4 BA-validation iteration 2: BA must fix 3 items: (1) Update AC6 THEN clause from 'uncapped_delta == 101' to 'unclamped_score == 101'. Also update Technical Hints test_AC4 note to use 'unclamped_score'. (2) Rewrite M8 to: 'Preserve AC_UID = "7d4395a2a5e37239" and AC_TYPE = "hook" constants verbatim. Rewrite test body AND docstring to lifecycle JSONL semantics with updated GIVEN/WHEN/THEN (delta==1, unclamped_score==101).' Remove 'only the assertion values change' sentence from Technical Hints. (3) Add AC10: GIVEN malformed JSONL in lifecycle.jsonl / WHEN score-inject.sh invoked / THEN exit code 1, non-empty stderr, no INJECTION_PROOF in stdout.

self_verification.evidence_summary: OBJ-1 resolution evidenced by ticket JSONL schema section showing 'reason' + M1 explicit rename + AC1 THEN clause 'No field named note'. OBJ-2 resolution evidenced by JSONL schema now showing exactly 8 fields + advisory-only note for event_id/seq/prev_event_id. OBJ-3 resolution evidenced by M1 'Do NOT retain agent-scores.json write' + M3 score-inject.sh reads lifecycle.jsonl. OBJ-4 resolution evidenced by M5 eliminated + git check-ignore -v confirming .gitignore:46:logs/* already covers lock file + AC9 uses --error-unmatch flag. NEW-1 evidenced by reading ticket line 251 (AC6: uncapped_delta) + ticket line 88 (M8: unclamped_score) + context JSON success_criteria (unclamped_score) + score-update.sh line 170 (uncapped_delta). NEW-2 evidenced by reading M8 three contradictory directives + test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py lines 15-19 docstring content. NEW-3 evidenced by reading test-inject-branches.sh lines 119-128 (all 3 branches expect pass) + M6 text + AC1-AC9 scan (no exit-code-1 AC).
